  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Severe water hammer (hydraulic shock) occurs immediately after the fixture valve closes.",
    "Lack of adequate pipe securing or missing water hammer arrestors near quick-closing valves."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install water hammer arrestors at the fixture valves.",
    "Secure loose PEX and copper piping with approved hangers and straps to prevent movement."
  ],
  "rationale": "The audio recording clearly captures a valve closing followed by a loud, distinct metallic banging sound characteristic of water hammer. This hydraulic shock can damage joints, fittings, and appliances over time, violating standard plumbing practices and code requirements for controlling pressure surges.",
  "references": [
    "IPC Section 604.9 (Water hammer control)",
    "UPC Section 609.10 (Water Hammer)"
  ]
}
